items.question: What is the purpose of each split in a train/validation/test partition?
options: All three are used in fit() for stability
Train fits parameters; validation selects models; test gives final held-out estimate
Train and validation fit; test is optional decoration
Test fits parameters; train is for documentation only
items.answer: Train fits parameters; validation selects models; test gives final held-out estimate
items.explanation: Each partition has a distinct role — only train data updates model parameters.

items.question: Why should hyperparameter tuning use validation instead of test metrics?
options: Test labels are always missing
Repeated test-set tuning leaks information and biases the final score
Validation rows are larger than test rows
Test data trains faster on GPU
items.answer: Repeated test-set tuning leaks information and biases the final score
items.explanation: The test set must stay locked — validation absorbs iterative model selection.

items.question: When should you stratify a train/val/test split?
options: Only for regression targets
When class proportions matter, especially with imbalanced classification
Never — stratification causes leakage
Only when using deep learning
items.answer: When class proportions matter, especially with imbalanced classification
items.explanation: Stratification keeps rare classes represented in every partition for meaningful metrics.

items.question: What is wrong with fitting StandardScaler on all data before splitting?
options: It slows inference
Hold-out rows influenced training statistics — metrics are optimistically biased
StandardScaler requires GPU
It removes the need for a test set
items.answer: Hold-out rows influenced training statistics — metrics are optimistically biased
items.explanation: Preprocessing must learn from train only so validation and test stay truly unseen.

items.question: Which split strategy fits a daily sales forecasting problem?
options: Random shuffle of all rows
Temporal split: train on earlier dates, validate/test on later dates
Duplicate the test set into training for balance
Use only cross-validation with no test set ever
items.answer: Temporal split: train on earlier dates, validate/test on later dates
items.explanation: Time-ordered data requires chronological hold-out — random shuffles leak future into training.

items.question: A project reports 0.93 test accuracy after trying 50 models ranked by test score. What is the concern?
options: The model is certainly production-ready
The test set was used for selection — the 0.93 estimate is likely optimistic
Fifty models is too few for statistical significance
Accuracy is the wrong metric for all problems
items.answer: The test set was used for selection — the 0.93 estimate is likely optimistic
items.explanation: Test data participated in tuning — it is no longer an unbiased generalization sample.
